name: Coroutines & Generators
def: Functions that can suspend and later resume, preserving local state between yields.
problem: Express cooperative multitasking, lazy sequences and async flows without OS threads.
mechanism: A coroutine yields control (and optionally a value) and resumes where it left off; the compiler turns it into a resumable state machine.
limits: Cooperative — one coroutine can block others
failures: A blocking call inside a coroutine stalling the scheduler
myths: "Coroutines = threads" — they are cooperative and usually single-threaded unless scheduled across cores
